title: Sour Crunchy Middle Eastern Lamb Kebabs
description: A flavorful and crunchy Middle Eastern-inspired dish featuring lamb kebabs, perfect for special occasions. The combination of sumac, pomegranate molasses, and ground nut oil alternatives creates a unique and delicious flavor profile.
instructions: Preheat the grill to medium-high heat (200°C/400°F).
In a large bowl, combine lamb mince, sumac, pomegranate molasses, lemon juice, salt, and black pepper. Mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Thread the lamb mixture onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the grill with olive oil to prevent sticking.
Grill the kebabs for 4-5 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 65°C/150°F for medium-rare.
Warm the pita bread by wrapping it in foil and heating it on the grill for a few minutes.
Assemble the kebabs by slicing them and serving in the warm pita bread with pickled turnips and a drizzle of pomegranate molasses.
Consider serving with additional sides, such as roasted vegetables or a simple green salad.
